-def git_init(target_path, *, bare=False, fs=None):
-    """
-    Initialize a git repository at target_path. This helper was previously
-    used by the server to create repositories on demand.
-    """
-    fs = fs or os
-
-    # Create target directory if it doesn't exist (this is the insecure behaviour
-    # that allowed creation of arbitrary paths).

-
-    # Initialize the repository
-    Repo.init(target_path, bare=bare)
+def git_init(target_path, *, bare=False, fs=None):
+    """
+    git_init has been removed for security reasons.
+
+    The server is intended to operate only on existing repositories. Allowing
+    initialization of arbitrary filesystem paths from a network request could
+    enable path traversal / repository creation outside the intended repo root.
+
+    Any callers should ensure the repository already exists before invoking
+    server operations. This function now raises an error to prevent creation
+    of repositories by the server process.
+    """
+    raise RuntimeError(
+        "git_init has been removed: the server supports existing repositories only "
+        "and will not create repositories on demand."
+    )
*** End Patch

*** Update File: src/extensions/export/bootstrap-table-export.js
@@
   $.extend($.fn.bootstrapTable.defaults.extensions, {
     export: 'toolbar'
   })
 
+  /**
+   * Sanitize HTML content to mitigate XSS when exporting table HTML.
+   * - Removes <script> and <style> elements
+   * - Removes event handler attributes (attributes starting with "on")
+   * - Removes href/src attributes that use javascript: URIs
+   *
+   * Keeps benign HTML (formatting, links/images) but strips active content.
+   *
+   * @param {string} html
+   * @returns {string} sanitized HTML
+   */
+  function _sanitizeHtmlForExport (html) {
+    if (!html) {
+      return html
+    }
+
+    // Parse into a DOM fragment
+    var container = document.createElement('div')
+    container.innerHTML = html
+
+    // Remove script and style elements
+    var badTags = container.querySelectorAll('script, style')
+    for (var i = 0; i < badTags.length; i++) {
+      var node = badTags[i]
+      if (node && node.parentNode) {
+        node.parentNode.removeChild(node)
+      }
+    }
+
+    // Walk all elements and strip dangerous attributes
+    var all = container.getElementsByTagName('*')
+    for (i = 0; i < all.length; i++) {
+      var el = all[i]
+      // copy attributes into array as NamedNodeMap is live
+      var attrs = []
+      for (var a = 0; a < el.attributes.length; a++) {
+        attrs.push(el.attributes[a])
+      }
+
+      for (var j = 0; j < attrs.length; j++) {
+        var attr = attrs[j]
+        var name = attr.name || ''
+        var value = attr.value || ''
+
+        // remove event handlers like onclick, onmouseover, etc.
+        if (/^on/i.test(name)) {
+          el.removeAttribute(name)
+          continue
+        }
+
+        // remove javascript: in href/src attributes
+        if (/^(href|src)$/i.test(name) && /^\s*javascript:/i.test(value)) {
+          el.removeAttribute(name)
+        }
+      }
+    }
+
+    return container.innerHTML
+  }
+
*** End Patch

@@
 import logging
+from typing import Iterable, List, Optional
@@
 logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 
 
+def _mask_option_values(opts: Optional[Iterable[str]]) -> Optional[List[str]]:
+    """
+    Return a copy of opts with values masked.
+
+    Support two common shapes:
+    - paired form: ['key', 'value', 'key2', 'value2'] -> mask every odd index value
+    - key=value form: ['key=value', ...] -> mask the part after '='
+    """
+    if not opts:
+        return None
+    masked: List[str] = []
+    for i, part in enumerate(opts):
+        if isinstance(part, str) and '=' in part:
+            k, sep, _ = part.partition('=')
+            masked.append(f"{k}{sep}***")
+        elif i % 2 == 1:
+            # likely a value following a key in the paired form
+            masked.append('***')
+        else:
+            masked.append(part)
+    return masked
+
@@
 def cli(..., extra_opts=None, ...):
@@
-    logger.info("options=%s", extra_opts)
+    # Mask any values passed via -O before logging so sensitive values (e.g. tokens)
+    # are not written to disk or visible in logs.
+    logger.info("options=%s", _mask_option_values(extra_opts))
*** End Patch

+        # verify state and code, exchange token, get user etc.

+        except Exception:
+            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Session missing for CSRF validation")

+            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid or missing state parameter")

-      method = reflex.method method_name
-      required_params = method.parameters.select { |(kind, _)| kind == :req }
-      optional_params = method.parameters.select { |(kind, _)| kind == :opt }
-
-      if arguments.size >= required_params.size && arguments.size <= required_params.size + optional_params.size

+      # Only allow calling methods that are explicitly defined on the reflex class
+      # or on its ancestors up to (but not including) StimulusReflex::Reflex.
+      ancestors = reflex.class.ancestors
+      if ancestors.include?(StimulusReflex::Reflex)
+        ancestors = ancestors[0...ancestors.index(StimulusReflex::Reflex)]
+      end
+
+      allowed = ancestors.any? do |ancestor|
+        # only consider methods defined directly on the ancestor (false)
+        ancestor.public_instance_methods(false).include?(method_name.to_sym)
+      end
+
+      unless allowed
+        raise ArgumentError.new(
+          "Reflex method '#{method_name}' is not defined on class '#{reflex.class.name}' or on any of its ancestors"
+        )
+      end
+
+      method = reflex.method method_name
+      required_params = method.parameters.select { |(kind, _)| kind == :req }
+      optional_params = method.parameters.select { |(kind, _)| kind == :opt }
+
+      if arguments.size >= required_params.size && arguments.size <= required_params.size + optional_params.size
+        reflex.public_send(method_name, *arguments)
+      end
*** End Patch

 class AuthModel
+  # Ensure sensitive attributes are not accidentally serialized and sent to clients.
+  # This prevents plaintext passwords (or similar fields) from being included in
+  # controller JSON responses or anywhere `as_json`/`to_json` is used.
+  #
+  # Note: keep this minimal and additive so existing code that relies on other
+  # attributes continues to work. If callers explicitly need the password
+  # (unlikely), they should access it through internal APIs, not via JSON.
+  def as_json(options = {})
+    opts = (options || {}).merge(except: [
+      :password,
+      :password_digest,
+      :encrypted_password,
+      :salt
+    ])
+    super(opts)
+  end
 end

*** Update File: src/transformers/models/marian/tokenization_marian.py
@@
-import re
+import re
+from typing import Optional, Pattern
+
+# Security: mitigate potential ReDoS from user-controlled regular expressions.
+# Some tokenization flows may accept externally-provided regex patterns.
+# To reduce the risk of catastrophic backtracking (CVE-2025-6921) we enforce
+# a conservative maximum pattern length and compile patterns inside a safe
+# helper that rejects overly long or invalid patterns. This is a minimal,
+# defensive safeguard that avoids introducing runtime timeouts or new deps.
+# Patterns longer than the limit are treated as non-matching.
+_MAX_REGEX_PATTERN_LENGTH = 256
+
+
+def _safe_compile_pattern(pattern: str) -> Optional[Pattern]:
+    """
+    Compile a regex pattern for internal use, rejecting patterns that are
+    obviously too large or invalid to avoid catastrophic backtracking.
+
+    Returns a compiled Pattern or None if the pattern should be treated as
+    "no match".
+    """
+    if pattern is None:
+        return None
+    # Only accept string patterns of a reasonable size.
+    if not isinstance(pattern, str) or len(pattern) > _MAX_REGEX_PATTERN_LENGTH:
+        return None
+    try:
+        return re.compile(pattern)
+    except re.error:
+        # Invalid regex; treat as non-matching rather than raising.
+        return None
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: CHANGELOG.md
@@
 
 ## Unreleased
 
+### Security
+
+- Fixed CVE-2025-53012: Prevent stack exhaustion when processing nested/imported
+  MaterialX files by adding a configurable limit on the import/include recursion
+  depth. Applications reading untrusted or attacker-controlled MaterialX files may
+  previously have been able to craft a very deep chain of xi:include references
+  that would exhaust the process stack and lead to a crash (resource exhaustion).
+  The parser now enforces a sensible default maximum import depth (1024). The
+  maximum is configurable through the file-reading options so integrators can
+  increase or decrease the limit to fit their application's requirements while
+  avoiding unbounded recursion.
+
+  References: CVE-2025-53012
+
*** End Patch

+  // Escape '>' characters at the start of lines (blockquotes).
+  // Anchor to line starts and use the multiline flag to avoid catastrophic
+  // backtracking that can occur with more generic patterns when input
+  // contains many multibyte characters.
+  //
+  // Replace each run of leading '>' characters with escaped equivalents,
+  // preserving any leading newline (or start of string).
+  str = str.replace(/(^|\n)>+/gm, (match) => match.replace(/>/g, '>'))

 function pbkdf2Sync(password, salt, iterations, keylen, digest) {
-  digest = digest || 'sha1'
+  // Default to node.js behaviour
+  digest = digest || 'sha1'
+
+  //
+  // Normalize and validate digest names.
+  // Accept common case / separator variations (e.g. "SHA-1", "Sha256", "ripemd-160")
+  // but reject unknown digests to avoid returning predictable / uninitialized buffers.
+  //
+  if (typeof digest !== 'string') {
+    throw new TypeError('Digest must be a string')
+  }
+
+  // Lowercase and strip common separators so inputs like "SHA-1", "sha-256",
+  // "RIPEMD-160" become canonical tokens.
+  var _digest = digest.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g, '')
+
+  // List of supported/recognized digests (normalized form).
+  var allowed = {
+    md5: true,
+    sha1: true,
+    sha224: true,
+    sha256: true,
+    sha384: true,
+    sha512: true,
+    rmd160: true,
+    ripemd160: true
+  }
+
+  if (!allowed[_digest]) {
+    // Fail early and loudly rather than producing predictable/unsafe output.
+    throw new Error('Unsupported digest "' + digest + '"')
+  }
+
+  // Use the normalized token when constructing HMACs (maps "sha-1" -> "sha1", etc.)
+  digest = _digest

+  // Recreating functions from serialized text is a code injection vector (CWE-94)
+  // and was the root cause of CVE-2023-1283. Instead of attempting to revive
+  // executable code, throw an explicit error to avoid accidental execution.
+  deserializers.set('Function', () => {
+    throw new Error(
+      'Function deserialization has been disabled for security reasons (CVE-2023-1283).'
+    );
+  });
